1	package federation
2	
3	import (
4		"bytes"
5		"context"
6		"crypto/tls"
7		"encoding/json"
8		"errors"
9		"fmt"
10		"io"
11		"log"
12		"net/http"
13		"net/url"
14		"strings"
15		"sync"
16		"time"
17	)
18	
19	// DefaultFanOutMaxConcurrency caps the number of in-flight per-spoke requests.
20	const DefaultFanOutMaxConcurrency = 8
21	
22	// DefaultFanOutPerNodeTimeout is the default per-node deadline for one fan-out call.
23	const DefaultFanOutPerNodeTimeout = 5 * time.Second
24	
25	// SkipReason is a short, machine-readable code describing why a spoke was
26	// skipped before any HTTP call was issued.
27	type SkipReason string
28	
29	const (
30		// SkipReasonIncompatibleVersion indicates the version handshake against the
31		// hub's own version+schema rejected this spoke (e.g. major mismatch,
32		// schema mismatch, unparseable ddx_version, …). The Handshake reason is
33		// included in the logged message.
34		SkipReasonIncompatibleVersion SkipReason = "incompatible_version"
35		// SkipReasonMissingCapability indicates the spoke does not advertise one of
36		// the capabilities required for this query.
37		SkipReasonMissingCapability SkipReason = "missing_capability"
38		// SkipReasonInvalidURL indicates the spoke's persisted URL did not parse.
39		SkipReasonInvalidURL SkipReason = "invalid_url"
40	)
41	
42	// NodeOutcomeKind describes how a single spoke contributed (or did not
43	// contribute) to a fan-out call. The hub uses this to update per-spoke status
44	// distinctly: offline (immediate transport failure on this fan-out) vs stale
45	// (heartbeat-overdue badge from the registry) vs ok.
46	type NodeOutcomeKind string
47	
48	const (
49		// OutcomeOK means the spoke returned a response (any HTTP 2xx).
50		OutcomeOK NodeOutcomeKind = "ok"
51		// OutcomeOffline means the transport itself failed: connection refused,
52		// DNS error, TLS handshake failed, mid-response disconnect, etc. The hub
53		// flips the spoke's registry status to StatusOffline on this outcome.
54		OutcomeOffline NodeOutcomeKind = "offline"
55		// OutcomeTimeout means the per-node timeout fired before a response was
56		// fully received. Distinct from offline so callers can keep the spoke's
57		// last-known status (likely stale) rather than promote it to offline.
58		OutcomeTimeout NodeOutcomeKind = "timeout"
59		// OutcomeHTTPError means the spoke responded with a non-2xx status. The
60		// transport worked, so we do not flip to offline; the body is captured.
61		OutcomeHTTPError NodeOutcomeKind = "http_error"
62		// OutcomeSkipped means the spoke was filtered out before fan-out; see
63		// NodeResult.SkipReason.
64		OutcomeSkipped NodeOutcomeKind = "skipped"
65	)
66	
67	// FanOutRequest is the GraphQL payload to send to every active spoke.
68	type FanOutRequest struct {
69		Query         string         `json:"query"`
70		OperationName string         `json:"operationName,omitempty"`
71		Variables     map[string]any `json:"variables,omitempty"`
72		// RequiredCapabilities, if non-empty, filters out spokes that do not
73		// advertise every named capability in their SpokeRecord.Capabilities.
74		RequiredCapabilities []string `json:"-"`
75	}
76	
77	// NodeResult is a single spoke's contribution to a fan-out call.
78	type NodeResult struct {
79		NodeID string
80		URL    string
81		// Status, when non-empty, is the registry status the hub should record for
82		// this spoke as a result of this fan-out (StatusOffline on transport
83		// failure). Empty means "do not change registry status".
84		Status SpokeStatus
85		// Outcome classifies how this spoke contributed.
86		Outcome NodeOutcomeKind
87		// Response is the raw GraphQL response body for OutcomeOK. The hub merges
88		// it into the federated response shape. Empty otherwise.
89		Response json.RawMessage
90		// HTTPStatus is the HTTP status code seen, or 0 on transport failure.
91		HTTPStatus int
92		// Err is the underlying error for non-OK outcomes.
93		Err error
94		// SkipReason is set when Outcome == OutcomeSkipped.
95		SkipReason SkipReason
96		// SkipDetail is a free-form human message accompanying SkipReason.
97		SkipDetail string
98		// Duration is the wall time spent on this spoke's call.
99		Duration time.Duration
100	}
101	
102	// FanOutResult is the merged outcome of a fan-out call.
103	type FanOutResult struct {
104		// Responses holds one entry per spoke that returned 2xx, keyed by node_id.
105		// The value is the raw GraphQL response body.
106		Responses map[string]json.RawMessage
107		// Errors holds one entry per spoke that produced any non-OK outcome
108		// (offline, timeout, http_error). Skipped spokes are NOT in this map —
109		// they appear in Skipped instead. Keyed by node_id.
110		Errors map[string]error
111		// Skipped holds one entry per spoke filtered out before fan-out, keyed by
112		// node_id, with the SkipReason as the value.
113		Skipped map[string]SkipReason
114		// StatusUpdates lists the (node_id → new status) changes the hub should
115		// apply to its registry as a side effect of this fan-out. Currently only
116		// transport-level failures (StatusOffline) are emitted here; heartbeat-
117		// driven status (stale) is left to the registry's own time-based reconcile.
118		StatusUpdates map[string]SpokeStatus
119		// Nodes is the per-node detail (every spoke considered, including skipped).
120		// Order matches the input registry order for deterministic test output.
121		Nodes []NodeResult
122	}
123	
124	// FanOutClient executes fan-out GraphQL queries across a federation registry.
125	type FanOutClient struct {
126		// HTTPClient is used for spoke /graphql calls. Nil → a default client that
127		// accepts self-signed TLS certs (matches the spoke server's auto-cert).
128		HTTPClient *http.Client
129		// MaxConcurrency caps concurrent in-flight requests. ≤0 → default.
130		MaxConcurrency int
131		// PerNodeTimeout is the per-spoke deadline. ≤0 → default.
132		PerNodeTimeout time.Duration
133		// HubDDxVersion / HubSchemaVersion drive the version handshake gate.
134		// Empty → version filter is skipped (test convenience).
135		HubDDxVersion    string
136		HubSchemaVersion string
137		// Logger receives info/warn lines; nil → log.Printf.
138		Logger func(format string, args ...any)
139		// Now is the time source used for Duration measurement. Nil → time.Now.
140		Now func() time.Time
141	}
142	
143	// NewFanOutClient builds a FanOutClient with defaults filled in.
144	func NewFanOutClient() *FanOutClient {
145		return &FanOutClient{
146			HTTPClient: &http.Client{
147				// Per-request deadlines drive cancellation; this is just a safety
148				// net for misbehaving servers that never close the connection.
149				Timeout: 30 * time.Second,
150				Transport: &http.Transport{
151					TLSClientConfig: &tls.Config{InsecureSkipVerify: true}, //nolint:gosec
152				},
153			},
154			MaxConcurrency: DefaultFanOutMaxConcurrency,
155			PerNodeTimeout: DefaultFanOutPerNodeTimeout,
156		}
157	}
158	
159	func (c *FanOutClient) logf(format string, args ...any) {
160		if c.Logger != nil {
161			c.Logger(format, args...)
162			return
163		}
164		log.Printf(format, args...)
165	}
166	
167	func (c *FanOutClient) now() time.Time {
168		if c.Now != nil {
169			return c.Now()
170		}
171		return time.Now()
172	}
173	
174	func (c *FanOutClient) maxConcurrency() int {
175		if c.MaxConcurrency <= 0 {
176			return DefaultFanOutMaxConcurrency
177		}
178		return c.MaxConcurrency
179	}
180	
181	func (c *FanOutClient) perNodeTimeout() time.Duration {
182		if c.PerNodeTimeout <= 0 {
183			return DefaultFanOutPerNodeTimeout
184		}
185		return c.PerNodeTimeout
186	}
187	
188	func (c *FanOutClient) httpClient() *http.Client {
189		if c.HTTPClient != nil {
190			return c.HTTPClient
191		}
192		return http.DefaultClient
193	}
194	
195	// Execute fans out req across spokes. Returns a merged FanOutResult; never
196	// returns a non-nil error for per-spoke failures — those are surfaced through
197	// FanOutResult.Errors. A non-nil error is reserved for misuse (e.g. nil req).
198	//
199	// The set of spokes considered is the input slice. Callers typically pass the
200	// hub registry's spokes filtered by Status == active|stale (offline/degraded
201	// handling depends on policy and is left to the caller). Skipped spokes still
202	// appear in the per-node Nodes detail with OutcomeSkipped + SkipReason.
203	//
204	// Cancellation: if ctx is canceled, in-flight goroutines abort their HTTP
205	// calls and return OutcomeOffline (with ctx.Err() as cause); callers that want
206	// to distinguish caller-cancellation from spoke failure should check ctx.Err()
207	// after Execute returns.
208	func (c *FanOutClient) Execute(ctx context.Context, spokes []SpokeRecord, req *FanOutRequest) (*FanOutResult, error) {
209		if req == nil {
210			return nil, errors.New("federation: fanout request is nil")
211		}
212		if strings.TrimSpace(req.Query) == "" {
213			return nil, errors.New("federation: fanout request query is empty")
214		}
215	
216		body, err := json.Marshal(struct {
217			Query         string         `json:"query"`
218			OperationName string         `json:"operationName,omitempty"`
219			Variables     map[string]any `json:"variables,omitempty"`
220		}{req.Query, req.OperationName, req.Variables})
221		if err != nil {
222			return nil, fmt.Errorf("federation: marshal fanout query: %w", err)
223		}
224	
225		results := make([]NodeResult, len(spokes))
226		sem := make(chan struct{}, c.maxConcurrency())
227		var wg sync.WaitGroup
228	
229		for i := range spokes {
230			i := i
231			s := spokes[i]
232			// Pre-flight filters: version, capability, URL.
233			if skip, reason, detail := c.preflightSkip(s, req.RequiredCapabilities); skip {
234				c.logf("federation fanout: skip node_id=%q reason=%s detail=%q", s.NodeID, reason, detail)
235				results[i] = NodeResult{
236					NodeID:     s.NodeID,
237					URL:        s.URL,
238					Outcome:    OutcomeSkipped,
239					SkipReason: reason,
240					SkipDetail: detail,
241				}
242				continue
243			}
244	
245			wg.Add(1)
246			go func() {
247				defer wg.Done()
248				select {
249				case sem <- struct{}{}:
250				case <-ctx.Done():
251					results[i] = NodeResult{
252						NodeID:  s.NodeID,
253						URL:     s.URL,
254						Outcome: OutcomeOffline,
255						Status:  StatusOffline,
256						Err:     ctx.Err(),
257					}
258					return
259				}
260				defer func() { <-sem }()
261				results[i] = c.callOne(ctx, s, body)
262			}()
263		}
264		wg.Wait()
265	
266		out := &FanOutResult{
267			Responses:     map[string]json.RawMessage{},
268			Errors:        map[string]error{},
269			Skipped:       map[string]SkipReason{},
270			StatusUpdates: map[string]SpokeStatus{},
271			Nodes:         results,
272		}
273		for _, r := range results {
274			switch r.Outcome {
275			case OutcomeOK:
276				out.Responses[r.NodeID] = r.Response
277			case OutcomeSkipped:
278				out.Skipped[r.NodeID] = r.SkipReason
279			default:
280				if r.Err != nil {
281					out.Errors[r.NodeID] = r.Err
282				} else {
283					out.Errors[r.NodeID] = fmt.Errorf("fanout: %s", r.Outcome)
284				}
285			}
286			if r.Status != "" {
287				out.StatusUpdates[r.NodeID] = r.Status
288			}
289		}
290		return out, nil
291	}
292	
293	// preflightSkip returns (true, reason, detail) when the spoke must be skipped
294	// before any HTTP call is issued.
295	func (c *FanOutClient) preflightSkip(s SpokeRecord, requiredCaps []string) (bool, SkipReason, string) {
296		if c.HubDDxVersion != "" && c.HubSchemaVersion != "" {
297			hs := Handshake(c.HubDDxVersion, c.HubSchemaVersion, s.DDxVersion, s.SchemaVersion)
298			if !hs.Accept {
299				return true, SkipReasonIncompatibleVersion, hs.Reason
300			}
301		}
302		if len(requiredCaps) > 0 {
303			have := map[string]struct{}{}
304			for _, c := range s.Capabilities {
305				// Skip the synthetic identity-fingerprint capability.
306				if strings.HasPrefix(c, "@identity:") {
307					continue
308				}
309				have[c] = struct{}{}
310			}
311			for _, want := range requiredCaps {
312				if _, ok := have[want]; !ok {
313					return true, SkipReasonMissingCapability, want
314				}
315			}
316		}
317		if strings.TrimSpace(s.URL) == "" {
318			return true, SkipReasonInvalidURL, "empty url"
319		}
320		if _, err := url.Parse(s.URL); err != nil {
321			return true, SkipReasonInvalidURL, err.Error()
322		}
323		return false, "", ""
324	}
325	
326	// callOne issues the GraphQL POST against one spoke under per-node timeout.
327	func (c *FanOutClient) callOne(ctx context.Context, s SpokeRecord, body []byte) NodeResult {
328		start := c.now()
329		cctx, cancel := context.WithTimeout(ctx, c.perNodeTimeout())
330		defer cancel()
331	
332		endpoint := strings.TrimRight(s.URL, "/") + "/graphql"
333		req, err := http.NewRequestWithContext(cctx, http.MethodPost, endpoint, bytes.NewReader(body))
334		if err != nil {
335			return NodeResult{
336				NodeID:   s.NodeID,
337				URL:      s.URL,
338				Outcome:  OutcomeOffline,
339				Status:   StatusOffline,
340				Err:      err,
341				Duration: c.now().Sub(start),
342			}
343		}
344		req.Header.Set("Content-Type", "application/json")
345		req.Header.Set("Accept", "application/json")
346	
347		resp, err := c.httpClient().Do(req)
348		if err != nil {
349			// Distinguish per-node timeout (deadline exceeded on cctx but parent
350			// ctx still live) from caller cancellation and from generic transport
351			// failure. Per-node timeout → keep last-known status; transport
352			// failure → flip to offline.
353			if cctx.Err() == context.DeadlineExceeded && ctx.Err() == nil {
354				return NodeResult{
355					NodeID:   s.NodeID,
356					URL:      s.URL,
357					Outcome:  OutcomeTimeout,
358					Err:      fmt.Errorf("per-node timeout after %s: %w", c.perNodeTimeout(), err),
359					Duration: c.now().Sub(start),
360				}
361			}
362			return NodeResult{
363				NodeID:   s.NodeID,
364				URL:      s.URL,
365				Outcome:  OutcomeOffline,
366				Status:   StatusOffline,
367				Err:      err,
368				Duration: c.now().Sub(start),
369			}
370		}
371		defer func() { _ = resp.Body.Close() }()
372	
373		buf, readErr := io.ReadAll(resp.Body)
374		if readErr != nil {
375			// Mid-response disconnect — transport-level failure → offline.
376			return NodeResult{
377				NodeID:     s.NodeID,
378				URL:        s.URL,
379				Outcome:    OutcomeOffline,
380				Status:     StatusOffline,
381				HTTPStatus: resp.StatusCode,
382				Err:        fmt.Errorf("read body: %w", readErr),
383				Duration:   c.now().Sub(start),
384			}
385		}
386	
387		if resp.StatusCode < 200 || resp.StatusCode >= 300 {
388			snippet := string(buf)
389			if len(snippet) > 256 {
390				snippet = snippet[:256] + "…"
391			}
392			return NodeResult{
393				NodeID:     s.NodeID,
394				URL:        s.URL,
395				Outcome:    OutcomeHTTPError,
396				HTTPStatus: resp.StatusCode,
397				Err:        fmt.Errorf("spoke returned HTTP %d: %s", resp.StatusCode, snippet),
398				Duration:   c.now().Sub(start),
399			}
400		}
401	
402		return NodeResult{
403			NodeID:     s.NodeID,
404			URL:        s.URL,
405			Outcome:    OutcomeOK,
406			HTTPStatus: resp.StatusCode,
407			Response:   json.RawMessage(buf),
408			Duration:   c.now().Sub(start),
409		}
410	}
411

124	// New creates a new DDx server bound to addr, serving data from workingDir.
125	func New(addr, workingDir string) *Server {
126		nodeName := resolveNodeName()
127		stateDir := serverAddrDir() // XDG-standard user-level dir, one per node
128		state := loadServerState(stateDir, nodeName)
129		state.workingDir = workingDir
130	
131		workers := NewWorkerManager(workingDir)
132		workers.ReconcileStaleWorkers()
133		beadHub := bead.NewWatcherHub(250 * time.Millisecond)
134		csrfStore, err := ddxgraphql.NewStaticCSRFTokenStore()
135		if err != nil {
136			// CSRF token generation should never fail outside of a broken
137			// crypto/rand. Fall back to a deterministic stub that rejects
138			// every request rather than failing open.
139			csrfStore = ddxgraphql.NewStaticCSRFTokenStoreWithToken("")
140		}
141		s := &Server{
142			Addr:                               addr,
143			WorkingDir:                         workingDir,
144			mux:                                http.NewServeMux(),
145			startTime:                          time.Now().UTC(),
146			workers:                            workers,
147			beadHub:                            beadHub,
148			state:                              state,
149			csrfTokens:                         csrfStore,
150			operatorPromptIdempotency:          ddxgraphql.NewMemoryIdempotencyCache(),
151			operatorPromptAutoApproveAllowlist: parseOperatorPromptAllowlistEnv(os.Getenv("DDX_OPERATOR_PROMPT_ALLOWLIST")),
152		}
153		state.coordinatorReg = workers.LandCoordinators
154	
155		// Register the server's own project immediately.
156		state.RegisterProject(workingDir)
157		_ = state.save()
158	
159		s.routes()
160		return s
161	}
162	
163	// parseOperatorPromptAllowlistEnv parses a comma-separated identity list
164	// (DDX_OPERATOR_PROMPT_ALLOWLIST) into a normalized allowlist. Empty entries
165	// are skipped; whitespace is trimmed. The literal sentinel "localhost"
166	// matches any localhost-kind actor.
167	func parseOperatorPromptAllowlistEnv(raw string) []string {
168		if raw = strings.TrimSpace(raw); raw == "" {
169			return nil
170		}
171		parts := strings.Split(raw, ",")
172		out := make([]string, 0, len(parts))
173		for _, p := range parts {
174			p = strings.TrimSpace(p)
175			if p == "" {
176				continue
177			}
178			out = append(out, p)
179		}
180		return out
181	}
182	
183	// SetOperatorPromptAutoApproveAllowlist replaces the in-memory allowlist
184	// used by operatorPromptSubmit (with autoApprove=true) and operatorPromptApprove.
185	// Tests use this to install a deterministic allowlist without juggling env vars.
186	func (s *Server) SetOperatorPromptAutoApproveAllowlist(allowlist []string) {
187		s.operatorPromptAutoApproveAllowlist = append([]string(nil), allowlist...)
188	}

190	// resolveNodeName returns DDX_NODE_NAME env, or the system hostname.
191	func resolveNodeName() string {
192		if n := os.Getenv("DDX_NODE_NAME"); n != "" {
193			return n
194		}
195		if h, err := os.Hostname(); err == nil && h != "" {
196			return h
197		}
198		return "unknown"
199	}

201	// Handler returns the server's HTTP handler (useful for testing).
202	func (s *Server) Handler() http.Handler {
203		return s.mux
204	}
205	
206	// State exposes the server's persistent state so adjacent packages (notably
207	// the perf harness) can register additional projects or query snapshots
208	// without reaching into unexported internals. The returned value MUST NOT be
209	// mutated concurrently with the server — it is the same pointer the server
210	// itself uses.
211	func (s *Server) State() *ServerState {
212		return s.state
213	}
214	
215	// RegisterProject adds (or refreshes) a project entry on the server's state
216	// and persists the updated state file. Returns the stored entry.
217	func (s *Server) RegisterProject(path string) ProjectEntry {
218		entry := s.state.RegisterProject(path)
219		_ = s.state.save()
220		return entry
221	}
222	
223	// Shutdown stops the server's background services: closes the bead lifecycle
224	// hub and stops all land coordinators. Returns the first error encountered.
225	// Both operations are idempotent and safe to call on an idle server.
226	func (s *Server) Shutdown() error {
227		// Best-effort spoke deregister so the hub registry does not show this
228		// node as stale after a graceful shutdown. Errors are swallowed inside
229		// ShutdownSpoke (the hub may legitimately be down).
230		if s.spoke != nil {
231			ctx, cancel := context.WithTimeout(context.Background(), 3*time.Second)
232			_ = s.ShutdownSpoke(ctx)
233			cancel()
234		}
235		s.beadHub.Close()
236		s.workers.LandCoordinators.StopAll()
237		return nil
238	}
239	
240	// ListenAndServe starts the server. If TsnetConfig.Enabled is true, a parallel
241	// Tailscale ts-net listener is started alongside the standard localhost listener.
242	func (s *Server) ListenAndServe() error {
243		if s.TsnetConfig != nil && s.TsnetConfig.Enabled {
244			errCh := make(chan error, 2)
245	
246			// Standard localhost listener
247			go func() {
248				errCh <- http.ListenAndServe(s.Addr, s.mux)
249			}()
250	
251			// ts-net listener
252			go func() {
253				errCh <- s.listenTsnet()
254			}()
255	
256			return <-errCh
257		}
258		return http.ListenAndServe(s.Addr, s.mux)
259	}
260	
261	// ListenAndServeTLS starts the server with TLS. If certFile and keyFile are
262	// empty, a self-signed certificate is auto-generated and cached under
263	// workingDir/.ddx/server/tls/.
264	func (s *Server) ListenAndServeTLS(certFile, keyFile string) error {
265		if certFile == "" || keyFile == "" {
266			tlsDir := filepath.Join(s.WorkingDir, ".ddx", "server", "tls")
267			var err error
268			certFile, keyFile, err = ensureSelfSignedCert(tlsDir)
269			if err != nil {
270				return fmt.Errorf("generating self-signed cert: %w", err)
271			}
272		}
273		s.writeAddrFile("https")
274		if s.TsnetConfig != nil && s.TsnetConfig.Enabled {
275			errCh := make(chan error, 2)
276			go func() {
277				errCh <- http.ListenAndServeTLS(s.Addr, certFile, keyFile, s.mux)
278			}()
279			go func() {
280				errCh <- s.listenTsnet()
281			}()
282			return <-errCh
283		}
284		return http.ListenAndServeTLS(s.Addr, certFile, keyFile, s.mux)
285	}
286	
287	// writeAddrFile writes the server's address to a user-level file so CLI
288	// clients can auto-discover it without configuration.
289	func (s *Server) writeAddrFile(scheme string) {

1	package server
2	
3	import (
4		"context"
5		"encoding/json"
6		"net/http"
7		"net/http/httptest"
8		"path/filepath"
9		"strings"

13		"github.com/DocumentDrivenDX/ddx/internal/federation"

15	
16	// hubFromServer wires the hub-mode Server into a real httptest.Server so the
17	// spoke under test can hit it over HTTP. The returned closer must be called.
18	//
19	// All requests against the returned URL will appear to originate from
20	// 127.0.0.1, so the hub's strict default trust gate accepts them — exactly
21	// what we want for the lifecycle integration tests.
22	func hubFromServer(t *testing.T, srv *Server) (string, func()) {
23		t.Helper()
24		httpSrv := httptest.NewServer(srv.Handler())
25		t.Cleanup(httpSrv.Close)
26		return httpSrv.URL, httpSrv.Close
27	}
28	
29	// spokeStatePath is the conventional per-test spoke-state location under
30	// XDG_DATA_HOME (which setupTestDir already isolates).
31	func spokeStatePath(t *testing.T) string {
32		t.Helper()
33		dir := t.TempDir()
34		return filepath.Join(dir, "spoke-state.json")
35	}
36	
37	// AC: --hub= flag registers on startup. Spoke appears in hub registry.
38	func TestSpokeRegistersOnStart(t *testing.T) {
39		hub := newHubServer(t, false)
40		hubURL, _ := hubFromServer(t, hub)
41	
42		dir := setupTestDir(t)
43		spoke := New(":0", dir)
44	
45		ctx, cancel := context.WithCancel(context.Background())
46		defer cancel()
47		if err := spoke.EnableSpokeMode(ctx, hubURL, "https://spoke-self:9000",
48			WithSpokeStatePath(spokeStatePath(t)),
49			WithSpokeHeartbeatInterval(time.Hour), // disable heartbeat noise
50			WithSpokeNodeID("spoke-1"),
51		); err != nil {
52			t.Fatalf("EnableSpokeMode: %v", err)
53		}
54	
55		snap := hub.HubFederationRegistrySnapshot()
56		if len(snap.Spokes) != 1 {
57			t.Fatalf("hub should hold 1 spoke after register, got %d", len(snap.Spokes))
58		}
59		if snap.Spokes[0].NodeID != "spoke-1" {
60			t.Errorf("hub registered wrong node_id: %q", snap.Spokes[0].NodeID)
61		}
62		if snap.Spokes[0].URL != "https://spoke-self:9000" {
63			t.Errorf("hub registered wrong URL: %q", snap.Spokes[0].URL)
64		}
65		if !spoke.SpokeMode {
66			t.Errorf("Server.SpokeMode should be true after EnableSpokeMode")
67		}
68	}
69	
70	// AC: Re-start with same node_id is idempotent (replaces, not duplicates).
71	func TestSpokeReregisterIsIdempotent(t *testing.T) {
72		hub := newHubServer(t, false)
73		hubURL, _ := hubFromServer(t, hub)
74		statePath := spokeStatePath(t)
75		dir := setupTestDir(t)
76	
77		for i := 0; i < 3; i++ {
78			s := New(":0", dir)
79			ctx, cancel := context.WithCancel(context.Background())
80			if err := s.EnableSpokeMode(ctx, hubURL, "https://spoke-self:9000",
81				WithSpokeStatePath(statePath),
82				WithSpokeHeartbeatInterval(time.Hour),
83				WithSpokeNodeID("spoke-x"),
84			); err != nil {
85				t.Fatalf("iteration %d: EnableSpokeMode: %v", i, err)
86			}
87			_ = s.ShutdownSpoke(ctx)
88			cancel()
89		}
90	
91		snap := hub.HubFederationRegistrySnapshot()
92		if len(snap.Spokes) > 1 {
93			t.Errorf("idempotent re-registration produced %d spokes; want ≤1", len(snap.Spokes))
94		}
95	}
96	
97	// AC: URL change between restarts triggers re-registration with new URL.
98	func TestSpokeReregistersOnURLChange(t *testing.T) {
99		hub := newHubServer(t, false)
100		hubURL, _ := hubFromServer(t, hub)
101		statePath := spokeStatePath(t)
102		dir := setupTestDir(t)
103	
104		s1 := New(":0", dir)
105		ctx1, cancel1 := context.WithCancel(context.Background())
106		if err := s1.EnableSpokeMode(ctx1, hubURL, "https://spoke-old:9000",
107			WithSpokeStatePath(statePath),
108			WithSpokeHeartbeatInterval(time.Hour),
109			WithSpokeNodeID("spoke-roving"),
110		); err != nil {
111			t.Fatalf("first start: %v", err)
112		}
113		cancel1()
114	
115		// Restart with a new URL.
116		s2 := New(":0", dir)
117		ctx2, cancel2 := context.WithCancel(context.Background())
118		defer cancel2()
119		if err := s2.EnableSpokeMode(ctx2, hubURL, "https://spoke-new:9000",
120			WithSpokeStatePath(statePath),
121			WithSpokeHeartbeatInterval(time.Hour),
122			WithSpokeNodeID("spoke-roving"),
123		); err != nil {
124			t.Fatalf("second start: %v", err)
125		}
126	
127		if !s2.SpokeAgent().URLChanged() {
128			t.Errorf("expected URLChanged=true after url change between restarts")
129		}
130		if got := s2.SpokeAgent().PreviousURL(); got != "https://spoke-old:9000" {
131			t.Errorf("PreviousURL: want old URL, got %q", got)
132		}
133	
134		snap := hub.HubFederationRegistrySnapshot()
135		if len(snap.Spokes) != 1 {
136			t.Fatalf("want 1 spoke after URL change, got %d", len(snap.Spokes))
137		}
138		if snap.Spokes[0].URL != "https://spoke-new:9000" {
139			t.Errorf("hub did not pick up new URL: got %q", snap.Spokes[0].URL)
140		}
141	}
142	
143	// AC: Heartbeat interval 30s ± jitter. We can't directly assert 30s in a
144	// test (it would be slow), but we verify (a) the default is 30s, (b) the
145	// jitter window is non-zero and bounded by the configured fraction, and
146	// (c) heartbeats actually fire and flip the spoke status to "active".
147	func TestSpokeHeartbeatJitterBounds(t *testing.T) {
148		if got := federation.DefaultHeartbeatInterval; got != 30*time.Second {
149			t.Errorf("DefaultHeartbeatInterval drift: %v want 30s", got)
150		}
151		cfg := federation.SpokeConfig{
152			NodeID:                  "n1",
153			URL:                     "https://x",
154			HubURL:                  "https://hub",
155			HeartbeatInterval:       1 * time.Second,
156			HeartbeatJitterFraction: 0.25,
157			StatePath:               filepath.Join(t.TempDir(), "spoke.json"),
158		}
159		sp, err := federation.NewSpoke(cfg)
160		if err != nil {
161			t.Fatalf("NewSpoke: %v", err)
162		}
163		// Sample many delays — none should fall outside ±25% of the base interval.
164		low := time.Duration(float64(cfg.HeartbeatInterval) * 0.75)
165		high := time.Duration(float64(cfg.HeartbeatInterval) * 1.25)
166		sawJitter := false
167		for i := 0; i < 200; i++ {
168			d := sp.NextHeartbeatDelay()
169			if d < low || d > high {
170				t.Fatalf("delay %v outside ±25%% bounds [%v,%v]", d, low, high)
171			}
172			if d != cfg.HeartbeatInterval {
173				sawJitter = true
174			}
175		}
176		if !sawJitter {
177			t.Errorf("expected jitter to vary delays; saw constant %v", cfg.HeartbeatInterval)
178		}
179	}
180	
181	// Heartbeats actually flip status to active end-to-end.
182	func TestSpokeHeartbeatFlipsStatusActive(t *testing.T) {
183		hub := newHubServer(t, false)
184		hubURL, _ := hubFromServer(t, hub)
185		dir := setupTestDir(t)
186	
187		spoke := New(":0", dir)
188		ctx, cancel := context.WithCancel(context.Background())
189		defer cancel()
190		if err := spoke.EnableSpokeMode(ctx, hubURL, "https://spoke:9000",
191			WithSpokeStatePath(spokeStatePath(t)),
192			WithSpokeHeartbeatInterval(20*time.Millisecond),
193			WithSpokeHeartbeatJitter(0.01),
194			WithSpokeNodeID("spoke-hb"),
195		); err != nil {
196			t.Fatalf("EnableSpokeMode: %v", err)
197		}
198	
199		// Wait for at least one heartbeat to land.
200		deadline := time.Now().Add(2 * time.Second)
